content: Just wrapped our AI discovery interviews across 6 departments. Key insight: the biggest automation wins aren't where you'd expect — back-office ops teams had 3x more viable use cases than engineering. Start with the people closest to the pain.

The best engineering managers I've worked with share one trait: they protect their team's focus like it's a finite resource. Because it is. Every "quick sync" and "fast question" has a hidden context-switch cost that compounds across the sprint.

Stop writing try/except blocks around everything. If you can't name the specific exception you're catching and explain why, you're just hiding bugs. Let it crash — a loud failure is better than a silent wrong answer.
